item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It kind of belabours its own point a lot near the end but I like it for what it is, simple imaginative stuff. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked it, it was easy enough to read. What made this scip for me though was the way the dehumanization of the Agent was handled. Subtle, but horrifying in its own way. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well this one is… unique… Never thought I'd see a Phantom Limb SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it, a few notes though.

SCP-3040-1 grumbles inaudibly for a few seconds, before Nathans clears her throat.

I feel it would be smoother to redact or otherwise note this section is skimmed due to it being about sensitive information related to the other agent/mission? It always feels a bit odd to me when otherwise unaffected characters grumble inaudibly during interviews.

Both are silent for a few moments, before the pen re-appears and SCP-3040-1 drops her arm.

I literally thought she dropped her arm, as in it fell off or otherwise was unattached? Just confused me for a second since we're talking about missing hands and prosthetics. Might just be me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…I like that idea a lot, actually. I might change it to that.

Thank you!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Accidentally trying to grab something falling on her left side? Like the same mug even maybe, so then it 'spills' on the recording and there's a notable decrease/lack of liquid in the mug when she puts it back down and they review the security tapes to prove her 'catching' it?
Though they probably wouldn't believe her about the amount of liquid in the cup.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A Good one,

Also here's the review about agent Sharlene from her fellow teammates

Looks 9/10
Personality 8/10
Combat 10/10
Hand 1/2

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Poor woman. First she loses a hand, then becomes a SCP. Working for the Foundation has​ its drawbacks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I knew I forgot something - Hell, I even planned on writing a tale for the mission itself. Thanks for pointing that out!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The length was one of the things I was worried about, especially the initial tests - which ones would you recommend I remove? Most simply detail the determination of the traits listed in the beginning, rather than new information, but I was unsure if I should remove them or not.

And how would you recommend she discover the anomaly? That was a part I really, really struggled with myself, and hearing some ways to fix it would be a huge help.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Originally I had a few tests showing that, but decided to cut them because it was too long with those tests in.

And thank you, I'll change that as well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Testing the properties with only things less than 20cm cubed doesn't prove that the maximum is 20cm cubed. Use an object over that limit to prove such.

Another slight nitpick is that if it was a vacuum in there, it'd be much colder than -40c. More around single digit Kelvin.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is really long and meandering, so much so that I barely pulled through till the end, and what was there wasn't worth the tediousness of 20-something tests and addenda. I have a feeling there's a less contrived way for her to have discovered the anomaly. -1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Stand Master: Sharlene Vangarre
Stand Name: [Left Hand Free]

Destructive Power C
Range D
Speed NA/N
Durability ∞
Precision B
Developmental Potential A

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hey, good job Ehksidian. If I might make one suggestion though:

SCP-3040-1 refers to former Agent Sharlene Vangarre, who lost her left hand due to a mission.

You might want to elaborate on this a little bit, even if it's just to say that the mission in question was Incident-████-██, or it involved SCP-████.
Great job!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Did so. Thanks for letting me know

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I recommend you delete your tags and wait till the tag team covers this SCP for you.

item.title: A Thank You (And attribution)
item.content:encoded:
Just wanted to give a quick thanks to everyone who helped me - DrWrong, Recursive, gibberingeloquence, deku_link, and everyone else who gave me critique. Sorry if I forgot your name, I'm…bad with names.

Image credit is http://www.publicdomainpictures.net/view-image.php?image=203982&picture=black-coffee-mug which is free to use and edit. I edited it myself.

Thank you.
